prompt: Illustrate the process of metamorphosis from a caterpillar to a butterfly as a sequence of futuristic bio-mechanical assembly stations, following the steps laid out in the provided cycle diagram.
prompt_refined:
Generate an infographic image depicting the metamorphosis of a caterpillar into a butterfly. The visual should be structured as a 4-step circular process, meticulously following the layout of the cycle diagram shown in image 1. Each of the four sections should represent a distinct futuristic bio-mechanical assembly station.

- The station located in the top-right position (corresponding to 'OPTION-01' in image 1) illustrates the initial assembly or growth of a caterpillar.
- The station in the bottom-right position (corresponding to 'OPTION-02' in image 1) showcases the transformation into a chrysalis, with bio-mechanical elements encasing the form.
- The station in the bottom-left position (corresponding to 'OPTION-03' in image 1) depicts the emergence of a butterfly from its chrysalis, with the machinery facilitating the final stage.
- The station in the top-left position (corresponding to 'OPTION-04' in image 1) displays a fully formed butterfly, presented as a finished product of the assembly line.

The entire composition should feature intricate glowing conduits connecting the stations, advanced robotic arms performing precise tasks, and sleek, high-tech machinery, all rendered in a clean, informational graphics style suitable for a modern presentation.
objects: A 4-step circular process infographic layout (as depicted in image 1)
Top-right futuristic bio-mechanical assembly station with a caterpillar
Bottom-right futuristic bio-mechanical assembly station with a chrysalis encased in bio-mechanical elements
Bottom-left futuristic bio-mechanical assembly station with a butterfly emerging from a chrysalis
Top-left futuristic bio-mechanical assembly station with a fully formed butterfly
Intricate glowing conduits connecting the stations
Advanced robotic arms performing precise tasks
Sleek, high-tech machinery within the stations
